message:
conformance(br-2e4w): enforce thin-shell wrapper contracts via integration tests

What changed
- Added `crates/fr-conformance/tests/script_wrapper_contract.rs`.
- Introduced contract tests that assert each shell wrapper delegates to the
  expected Rust binary command line and executes via `"${cmd[@]}"`.
- Added guard assertions that wrappers do not reintroduce legacy orchestration
  snippets (`~/.local/bin/rch exec --`, direct `hyperfine`, direct `strace`,
  or ad-hoc `while (($# > 0)); do` parsers).

Why
- The porting track moved orchestration logic into Rust binaries while keeping
  shell entrypoints for operator ergonomics.
- These tests prevent regression to script-heavy orchestration and keep wrappers
  intentionally thin over time.

Validation
- `cargo fmt --all` and `cargo fmt --check`
- `~/.local/bin/rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets`
- `~/.local/bin/rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `~/.local/bin/rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-conformance -- --nocapture`
- `ubs --only=rust crates/fr-conformance/tests/script_wrapper_contract.rs`
